title: Il virologo cinese che scoprì il genoma del Covid dorme in strada
en_title: The Chinese virologist who discovered the Covid genome sleeps in the street
en_content:
AGI - After the closure of his laboratory Zhang Yongzhen, the first scientist who shared with the world the genomic sequence of SARS-CoV-2, accelerating the development of vaccines, he now sleeps encamped outside his laboratory. The virologist decided to publicly contest the Shanghai Public Health Clinical Center, or SPHCC, of the University of Fudan, China last week. Zhang was photographed camping outside his workshop on Sunday, after the closure of the SPHCC, which issued public statements in which he states that the lab was one of the many who needed renovation and that provided Zhang's group with an alternative space.
According to posts published on social media on Zhang's personal account on Weibo, the institute gave the research group two days to leave, but initially the SPHCC did not specify where they should move.
<div </div
 
Later, Zhang stated that officials told his team to move to a lab that had no biosecurity conditions needed to preserve the samples, which contain unknown pathogens. Zhang's lab is a level 3 biosecurity lab. Zhang said he slept outside his lab, even in the rain. Social media posts include photos of him lying under the blankets. Monday Zhang described his situation as "terrible." "You don't know what I've lived," Zhang said. According to Chen Yanmei's social media account, he virologa at SPHCC and member of Zhang's team, the incomplete experiments of their students were now "impossible to save". Chen also wrote that he was camping, but inside the lab. On Tuesday's late evening, Zhang stated in a post that had been reached a provisional agreement with the SPHCC to resume normal research activity in the laboratory. In the post we read that Zhang will work with the center to transfer the lab and restart the search. In 2020, Zhang, along with Edward Holmes, a virologist at the University of Sydney in Australia, was the first scientist to share with the world the genome of SARS-CoV-2, publishing it on the Virological discussion forum. This act is widely accredited as one of the key factors that allowed rapid development of vaccines against COVID-19.
Since 2020, Zhang has received international awards and that year, Zhang has been included in the list of 10 of Nature, an annual list of people behind major developments in science, and in 2022 he received the Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum Knowledge Award, with a scholarship of $1 million. But Holmes, long-time collaborator of Zhang, says that Zhang's research production has declined since 2020. "Before then, Zhang had a wide network of research and collected samples from animals and people to study viral evolution," said Holmes. But since 2020, Zhang's work has largely covered the analysis of previously collected samples and Holmes' collaboration with Zhang has been less prolific. "He pushed the collaboration, but now there is nothing to collaborate on; he was unable to get any data," commented Holmes. "All I can do is offer support from afar," Holmes added. According to the Dimensions database, Zhang was co-author of 5 research articles in 2018, 9 in 2019 and 18 in 2020. But, the growing publishing rate fell to 3 in 2021 and 4 in 2022. In 2023 Zhang was co-author of 6 articles, none of which contained original data. Holmes believes that the closure of the lab is part of an attempt to disband Zhang for unauthorized data sharing. "It's amazing to see this situation," Holmes said. "It is unthinkable for me that a scientist of that caliber reigns out of his lab," Holmes continued. But, according to Yanzhong Huang, Chinese health policy specialist at the Council on Foreign Relations, the true nature of the dispute and protest are "involved in mystery". Fan Xiaohong, a doctor running the SPHCC, told Chinese newspaper Southern Weekly that Zhang's contract expired, but he refused to leave. In a social media post by Zhang we read that, although his contract with the SPHCC was formally expired in 2023, members of his team renewed their contracts with the hospital and the laboratory must still receive funding. Although the details of the controversy were not clarified, Dali Yang, a polythologist specializing in China at the University of Chicago in Illinois, believes that Zhang dominates outside his laboratory. "Many people who know him are baffled because he is really an international star," Yang.&nbsp said.

title: Due operai morti in due cantieri a Napoli
content: AGI - Due operai sono morti in due diversi incidenti sul lavoro nel Napoletano. Il primo caso a Lettere, intorno a mezzogiorno, in via Depugliano, un operaio di 57 anni è precipitato dal terzo piano di un palazzo attorno al quale era in allestimento un cantiere edile. A Casalnuovo, poi, questa mattina presso la clinica Villa dei fiori un operaio 60enne è morto in un cantiere in viale dei Tigli. Indagini in corso dei carabinieri in entrambi i casi per ricostruire la dinamica e la responsabilità.
en_title: Two workers dead in two yards in Naples
en_content: AGI - Two workers died in two different accidents at work in the Neapolitan. The first case in Lettere, around noon, in Via Depugliano, a 57-year-old worker fell from the third floor of a building around which a construction site was being set up. In Casalnuovo, then, this morning at the clinic Villa dei Fiori a 60-year-old worker died in a yard in viale dei Tigli. Investigations in progress of the Carabinieri in both cases to reconstruct dynamics and responsibility.

title: Permane l'instabilità su buona parte dell'Italia
content:
AGI - L'ultimo mese della primavera inizia all'insegna del maltempo su buona parte dell'Italia. Una circolazione depressionaria si posiziona infatti tra Atlantico ed Europa centro-occidentale interessando anche il Mediterraneo. Ancora tempo instabile tra oggi e domani su molte regioni con acquazzoni e temporali localmente anche di forte intensità. In arrivo anche aria più fresca che porterà un generale calo delle temperature su valori anche di 4-6 gradi al di sotto delle medie del periodo. Tra sabato e domenica però il tempo potrebbe prendere una piega più stabile grazie alla rimonta di un promontorio anticiclonico. Stabilità comunque non assoluta in quanto soprattutto al Nord potrebbero ancora presentarsi piogge e acquazzoni. Sono gli ultimi aggiornamenti del Centro Meteo Italiano che per la prossima settimana indicano una nuova fase instabile verso il Mediterraneo.

Previsioni del tempo di oggi

  • AL NORD Tempo instabile nel corso della giornata sulle regioni settentrionali con molte nuvole associate a piogge e acquazzoni sparsi sia al mattino che al pomeriggio, localmente anche a carattere di temporale. Ancora precipitazioni sparse in serata e nottata ma in graduale esaurimento.
 
  • AL CENTRO Condizioni di tempo instabile o perturbato sulle regioni del Centro con nuvolosità associata a piogge e temporali sparsi sia al mattino che al pomeriggio, a tratti anche intensi. In serata e nottata tempo in graduale miglioramento con precipitazioni in esaurimento.
 
  • AL SUD E SULLE ISOLE Tempo asciutto al mattino sulle regioni meridionali con cieli sereni o poco nuvolosi, locali piogge sulla Sardegna. Al pomeriggio precipitazioni in arrivo anche su Campania, Molise, Basilicata e Sicilia, più asciutto altrove. Tempo in miglioramento dalla serata salvo residue piogge su Campania e Calabria. Temperature minime e massime in generale diminuzione da Nord a Sud.

Previsioni del 3 maggio

  • AL NORD Tempo stabile al mattino con nuvolosità e schiarite, locali fenomeni sulle Alpi centro-orientale con neve oltre i 1800-1900 metri. Al pomeriggio piogge e temporali in sviluppo su Alpi e Appennini con neve oltre i 2000-2200 metri, più asciutto altrove. In serata locali sconfinamenti dalle Alpi verso le pianure. Migliora nella notte ma con ancora molte nubi.
 
  • AL CENTRO Al mattino piogge sui settori tirrenici, variabilità asciutta altrove e cieli sereni sui settori adriatici. Al pomeriggio instabilità in aumento con acquazzoni e temporali in sviluppo nelle zone interne e in sconfinamento sul versante adriatico. In serata tempo in generale miglioramento con esaurimento dei fenomeni ma ancora qualche disturbo nuvoloso.
 
  • AL SUD E SULLE ISOLE Al mattino nubi sparse con locali piogge sulla Campania, sereno o poco nuvoloso altrove. Al pomeriggio acquazzoni e temporali in sviluppo in Appennino con sconfinamenti sulle regioni adriatiche. Tempo in miglioramento dalla serata ma con ancora disturbi nuvolosi. Temperature minime in generale diminuzione, massime stabili o in lieve rialzo al Nord-Ovest e sulla Sardegna e in lieve calo sul resto d'Italia.
en_title: Still instability on most of Italy
en_content:
AGI - The last month of spring begins with bad weather on most of Italy. A pulsary circulation is located between the Atlantic and Western Europe, also affecting the Mediterranean. Still unstable time between today and tomorrow on many regions with local aquazzoni and storms also of strong intensity. Incoming also fresher air that will bring a general decrease of temperatures on values also of 4-6 degrees below the averages of the period. Between Saturday and Sunday, however, time could take a more stable turn thanks to the reassemble of an anticyclonic promontory. However, this is not an absolute stability, as rains and showers may still be present in the North. The latest updates are Centro Meteo Italiano that for next week indicate a new unstable phase towards the Mediterranean.

Today's time forecast <
  • AL NORD Unsteady weather during the day on the northern regions with many clouds associated with rains and showers spread both in the morning and in the afternoon, locally also with a temporal character. Still rainfall spread in the evening and night but in gradual exhaustion. < > AL CENTER Time conditions unstable or perturbed on the regions of the Centre with cloudyness associated with rains and thunderstorms spread both in the morning and in the afternoon, sometimes even intense. In the evening and night time in gradual improvement with rainfall in exhaustion. < > AL SUD AND ISOLE Dry weather in the morning on the southern regions with calm or unmarried skies, local rains on Sardinia. In the afternoon precipitations coming also on Campania, Molise, Basilicata and Sicily, dryer elsewhere. Time in improvement from the evening except rains on Campania and Calabria. Minimum and maximum temperatures in general decrease from North to South.
    • Forecasts of 3 May <
  • AL NORD Stable weather in the morning with cloudyness and lightening, local phenomena on the central-eastern Alps with snow over 1800-1900 meters. In the afternoon rains and storms in development on the Alps and Apennines with snow over 2000-2200 meters, dryer elsewhere. In the evening, the Alpine border to the plains. Improves in the night but with many more clouds.
    • AL SUD AND ISOLE In the morning clouds scattered with local rains on Campania, serene or little cloudy elsewhere. In the afternoon aquazzoni and temporal in development in Appennino with treasury over the Adriatic regions. Time improving from the evening but with still cloudy disorders. Minimum temperatures in general decrease, maximum stable or slightly rising to the North-West and Sardinia and in a slight decrease on the rest of Italy.

title: Salta la seduta della Commissione Antimafia che doveva ascoltare Emiliano
content:
AGI - La commissione parlamentare Antimafia ha sconvocato la seduta prevista per giovedì 2 maggio, nella quale era prevista l'audizione del Governatore della Puglia, Michele Emiliano. Sul sito della Camera si legge che la seduta "non avrà luogo". Emiliano, due giorni fa, aveva inviato una lettera alla presidente della Commissione parlamentare d'inchiesta Antimafia per comunicare la sua "indisponibilità" e come "causa di legittimo impedimento" aveva spiegato di avere "alle ore 10 la convocazione della Conferenza delle Regioni e alle successive ore 12.30 dalla Conferenza unificata convocata dal Ministro Calderoli".
Emiliano, nella stessa lettera, aveva aggiunto: "In ogni caso, essendo mio intendimento essere audito in commissione, comunico che sono disponibile in ogni momento dal 10 al 30 maggio, a patto che si sia concluso con il voto il dibattito nel Consiglio regionale della Puglia sulla fiducia richiesto dal centrodestra".
en_title: Skip the session of the Antimafia Commission that had to listen to Emiliano
en_content:
AGI - The parliamentary commission Antimafia has called for the session scheduled for Thursday 2 May, in which the audition of the Governor of Puglia, Michele Emiliano was planned. On the site of the Chamber we read that the session "will not take place". Emiliano, two days ago, had sent a letter to the president of the parliamentary commission of investigation Antimafia in order to communicate his "indisability" and as "case of legitimate impediment" he had explained to have "at 10 hours the convocation of the Conference of the Regions and at the next 12.30 hours from the unified conference convened by the Minister Calderoli".
Emiliano, in the same letter, added: "In any case, being my intention to be audited in committee, I am available at any time from 10 to 30 May, provided that the debate in the Regional Council of Puglia on the trust requested by the centre-right ended with the vote."
